t: About Mokshify - AI-First Software Engineering Studio, Hyderabad
d: The studio behind the film: what Mokshify is, what it is not, the numbers that are real, and how we think - an AI-first product engineering team in Hyderabad, India.
How Mokshify systems are actually built: production reference architecture, CI/CD with AI review, RAG pipeline, multi-tenant data design and security layers - with diagrams and explanations.
Measured, reproducible numbers: 97.7% frame payload reduction, 55 MB for a 914-frame film, sub-200KB page JS, 99.98% uptime - and an honest list of what we refuse to publish.
The Mokshify mark, full logo sheet, app icon, palette and typography - real downloadable files with two simple usage rules.
Engineering at Mokshify: small team, full ownership, AI-leveraged development, published standards and a production-first culture in Hyderabad. Introduce yourself with something you built.
A free 30-minute strategy call with Mokshify engineers: bring the problem, leave with an honest feasibility read, a scope suggestion and next steps - reply within 24 hours.
Reach an engineer directly: hello@mokshify.io, +91 93912 48520, WhatsApp - Hyderabad, India, replies within 24 hours. No ticket queues, no chatbots.
Why Flutter, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, Terraform, GitHub Actions, multi-provider AI and boring technology - ten ADRs with context, tradeoffs and revisit conditions.

Mokshify's DevOps: trunk-based git flow, CI/CD with AI review, Terraform infrastructure as code, Docker and Kubernetes, staged and blue-green deployments, monitoring, logging and one-command rollback.

t: Engineering at Mokshify - Library, Journal, Decisions, Benchmarks, Tools

Mokshify's engineering operations, published: code review, branch strategy, testing, deployment and rollback, monitoring, incident response, documentation, knowledge transfer and post-launch support.
Estimate your software project with Mokshify: duration, suggested team and engagement model, derived from our published delivery anchors - plus the cost drivers, honestly explained.
Answers about working with Mokshify: pricing and engagement models, timelines, technology choices, AI development, code ownership, communication and support.
The four questions we ask in week one: is the workflow frequent and measurable, does usable data exist, can mistakes be caught, and will anyone own it - with honest not-yet signals.

t: How to Choose a Software Company (and Questions to Ask)
d: What predicts a good outcome, eight questions to ask before hiring developers, and red flags from the inside - a guide useful even if you never hire us.
The checklist we run before any workload moves: honest inventory, risk-based sequencing, rehearsed state migration, parallel running, rollback-guarded cutover and deliberate decommissioning.
The four real cost drivers, three pricing models and their incentives, lifecycle budgeting and how to compare quotes - honest guidance without a magic number.
An MVP is a question with software attached: state the bet, choose the kill metric, test demand cheaply, build one workflow, and read the result honestly.

t: Why Software Projects Fail - Five Patterns
d: Projects rarely die of bad code: unwritten problems, v1-as-v3 scope, demo-data lies, deployment as afterthought, and status-deck silence - with what survival looks like.
Mokshify is an AI-first software engineering studio in Hyderabad. We turn startup ideas into world-class digital products — MVP sprints, product engineering, dedicated teams and AI transformation.
Institute platforms, course catalogues as structured data, and mobile-first enrolment flows - proven by a live Salesforce training platform competing from a tier-2 city.

Diagnostics platforms, patient portals, lab operations and AI with human checkpoints - healthcare software built with clinical seriousness, proven by a live diagnostics platform.
Who Mokshify works with: healthcare and diagnostics, fintech, real estate, education, startups, SMBs and enterprises - with live example projects in each.
Discovery platforms, project marketing and enquiry pipelines for high-trust property purchases - proven by a live platform for HMDA-approved plots near Hyderabad.

How mokshify.io practises accessibility: reduced-motion support, semantic structure, keyboard paths, contrast discipline and a no-JavaScript content path - with honest limits.
mokshify.io sets no cookies - no tracking, no ads, no analytics cookies, no banner. If that changes, this page changes first.
How to report a vulnerability to Mokshify: 24-hour acknowledgement, no legal threats for good-faith research, credit if you want it - and an honest note that no paid bounty exists.
What mokshify.io actually does with data: no tracking cookies, no forms to our servers, standard host logs, and email you control - written plainly.
Plain-language website terms: what you may do with this content, what it is and is not, how engagements differ from the website, and where liability actually lives.

t: Authentication Architecture - Tokens, Sessions and Audit
d: The authentication shape we ship: OIDC identity service, short-lived access tokens, rotating refresh tokens, separate authorization with audit logs - plus the honest session-cookie shortcut for MVPs.

t: Background Jobs and Message Queues with Redis
d: Getting slow work off the request path: idempotency, retries with backoff, dead-letter queues, job status as product surface - and when Redis queues beat a real broker.

t: Caching Strategy: Browser, CDN, Redis, PostgreSQL
d: A layered caching architecture with the rules that keep it sane: TTLs everywhere, stampede protection, never cache authorization - advantages, disadvantages and diagrams.

t: AWS vs Azure vs OCI vs GCP - An Honest Comparison
d: Written by a team that deploys to all four: what actually differs, what does not, the five-step decision procedure, and why boring architecture makes the choice reversible.
Long-form architecture writing with diagrams, tradeoffs and honest disadvantages: multi-tenancy, caching, background jobs, and the decisions behind them.

t: LLM Evaluation - Tests for Non-Deterministic Software
d: How to test AI features like software: real-case test sets, fact-level assertions, defensible scoring, and eval gates in CI - with per-use-case metrics for RAG, extraction and agents.

t: Monolith vs Microservices - The Honest Decision
d: When a modular monolith wins, when services earn their operational cost, the migration path between them, and the fashion trap - with diagram and defaults.

t: Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture - Row-Level vs Schema vs Database
d: Row-level, schema-per-tenant or database-per-tenant: advantages, disadvantages, tradeoffs and the promotion escape hatch - with diagrams, from Mokshify's production practice.

t: Observability - Logs, Metrics, Traces, Prioritised Honestly
d: Structured logs first, gating metrics second, traces when architecture earns them - plus alerting that respects humans and the real costs nobody budgets.

t: RAG Explained - Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Production
d: The RAG pipeline as we ship it: chunking that decides quality, pgvector in the product database, hybrid retrieval, citations as auditability - and when you should skip RAG entirely.
